textRuns.value: Across all sixteen months the inflation holds its shape — no broken month, no step-change. Each metric stays inside a tight band (most vary less than 10% around their own level), which is what a healthy, consistent feed looks like. The earliest months sit right alongside the most recent ones, so there's no onboarding distortion to discount.

Two things stand out, neither a problem. The person metrics (Users, PaS, Total Population) peaked together in mid-2025 — around 1.8 in May — then settled back to ~1.5–1.6; because unrelated metrics moved in step, this reads as a seasonal swing in how long people lingered, not a data fault. And the eye-contact metrics are the most variable line and have eased down over the period (from ~1.0 in mid-2025 to ~0.6–0.75 lately) — a drift in how the eye-contact extrapolation splits across the hours, still within tolerance but the one line worth keeping an eye on.
The practical read: nothing here needs action. This page is the early-warning system — if a line ever steps outside its band, that's the month to open the raw file and check the delivery.
